## [03:02 - 06:07]

There's the proactive test, which is going to view understanding that there's an opportunity and then there's the reactive test, which is kind of based off of the need. All right. So an example of a proactive test would be, you know, you have an area of your funnel that you've been trying to optimize for a while. A reactive test is when you have a campaign that is bombing and you need to make a difference, right? So most of the time, I think people think of testing and optimization as more of a reactive process. And I think that's a, that's kind of the gateway to getting into optimization. An example of this is we did something like that at digital marketer where we used our customer, we looked at our customer avatars and when we did our ad grid, we had different avatars and different hooks. And based on the content that was in the ad itself, we automatically changed the headline and subhead on the page to match the ad for that particular hook and avatar. That was an immediate boost in conversions for us and required no new landing pages.

## [27:27 - 28:45]

and then work that into their project plan for mass migration. So migration steps are as follows. Send pages in Envision with Notes to Outsource Group. Get page to the 80% plus completion level there and pass to an internal team member. Have project manager assemble page content in URLs for all new pages. When template is 100%, have internal team fill in content and make live. So that one right there, the one with the asterisk number three, you want that running at the same time that the page is being coded. Don't wait for it to be at 80% to start getting the project manager involved. Have them actively getting new copy, new images and those types of things because they're coming from creatives, which will take a little bit more time. But once the template is 100%, you'll have all the stuff you need to fill it in and you'll be able to launch a lot faster. All right, so that's really my entire method for prototyping new, drastically different pages, proving them and then mass migrating them using an outsourced in-house team. So hopefully you're going to be able to use this method as well and start to see a nice big lift in conversions and hopefully a drop in ad spend on your next campaign.
